title: Morning Bid: Chip chill, jobs heat and war
description: Reuters framed the US morning setup around a chip-sector rout, strong US jobs data, higher Fed hike odds, rising Treasury yields, and Middle East oil risk. SOX fell 10% Friday, Broadcom was down 20% over two days, Nasdaq was off about 4% into the weekend, and markets priced almost an 80% chance of a Fed hike by year-end after May payrolls rose 172,000 with +93,000 revisions.
key_points: Markets saw almost 80% chance of a Fed hike by year-end
Crude jumped over 4% on renewed Iran-Israel attacks
summary: Morning Bid: Chip chill, jobs heat and war — Reuters framed the US morning setup around a chip-sector rout, strong US jobs data, higher Fed hike odds, rising Treasury yields, and Middle East oil risk

title: Yields mixed after jobs data lifts Fed hike odds
description: Reuters reported fed funds futures pricing a 70% chance of a December hike after stronger jobs data. The 2-year yield fell 0.9bp to 4.153% after hitting a 15-month high Friday; the 10-year rose 1.4bp to 4.55%; the 2s10s curve steepened to 39.4bp. Treasury will sell $119B of coupon supply this week: $58B 3Y Tuesday, $39B 10Y Wednesday, $22B 30Y Thursday.
key_points: Fed funds futures show 70% December hike probability
summary: Yields mixed after jobs data lifts Fed hike odds — Reuters reported fed funds futures pricing a 70% chance of a December hike after stronger jobs data

title: Dollar eases as investors weigh Middle East hopes, rate outlook
description: Reuters said the dollar edged lower Tuesday as hopes for a Strait of Hormuz reopening reduced safe-haven demand, while rate expectations stayed hawkish. DXY was down 0.12% at 99.88; EURUSD rose 0.1% to $1.1545; USDJPY traded near 160.22; AUDUSD rose 0.14% to $0.7054. CME FedWatch showed a 70% chance of a Fed hike by December; markets also expected a 25bp ECB hike Thursday.
summary: Dollar eases as investors weigh Middle East hopes, rate outlook — Reuters said the dollar edged lower Tuesday as hopes for a Strait of Hormuz reopening reduced safe-haven demand, while rate expectations stayed hawkish

title: Gold steady as lower oil offsets US rate-hike fears
description: Reuters reported spot gold steady at $4,327.42/oz at 0850 GMT after a two-day slump, with August gold futures down 0.3% at $4,351.30. Traders saw more than a 70% chance of a December Fed hike. Gold closed below its 200-day moving average Friday for the first time since October 2023, which Citi called a negative technical signal.
key_points: Gold below 200DMA for first time since Oct 2023
May CPI due Wednesday, PPI Thursday
summary: Gold steady as lower oil offsets US rate-hike fears — Reuters reported spot gold steady at $4,327.42/oz at 0850 GMT after a two-day slump, with August gold futures down 0.3% at $4,351.30

title: Oil prices settle $1 higher after Iran and Israel say they have halted attacks
description: Reuters reported Brent settled up $1.16, or 1.3%, at $94.25/bbl and WTI up $0.76, or 0.8%, at $91.30/bbl Monday after rising more than 5% intraday on renewed Iran-Israel strikes before easing after both countries said attacks had halted. Since the conflict began just over 100 days ago, Brent was up about 31% and WTI about 37%; Brent previously peaked above $126/bbl in April.
key_points: Oil rose more than 5% intraday before paring
OPEC+ approved fourth output target increase in four months
summary: Oil prices settle $1 higher after Iran and Israel say they have halted attacks — Reuters reported Brent settled up $1.16, or 1.3%, at $94.25/bbl and WTI up $0.76, or 0.8%, at $91.30/bbl Monday after rising more than 5% intraday on renewed Iran-Isr...

title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Nvidia, Marvell, Flex and more
description: CNBC reported chip and AI-linked premarket rebounds: Nvidia +2.3% on an SK Hynix advanced-memory partnership for AI factory buildout; Marvell almost +9% and Flex +4% on S&P 500 additions; Micron +7%; Broadcom +3%; AMD +2.7%. Nurix surged 24% on a Roche collaboration.
key_points: NVDA +2.3% premarket on SK Hynix AI-memory partnership
summary: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Nvidia, Marvell, Flex and more — CNBC reported chip and AI-linked premarket rebounds: Nvidia +2.3% on an SK Hynix advanced-memory partnership for AI factory buildout; Marvell almost +9% and Flex +4% on S&...

title: Marvell to join S&P 500, stock jumps 10%
description: CNBC reported Marvell was up 10.4% shortly after Monday's open after S&P Global said it would join the S&P 500 on June 22 with Flex. Pool Corp and Campbell's are being removed. Marvell was up 210% YTD, had a $230B market cap, reported Q1 revenue of $2.4B, and had surged 32.5% on June 2 after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang called it the next trillion-dollar company.
summary: Marvell to join S&P 500, stock jumps 10% — CNBC reported Marvell was up 10.4% shortly after Monday's open after S&P Global said it would join the S&P 500 on June 22 with Flex

title: Yahoo Finance market snapshot: US stock futures climb as AI trade takes center stage
description: Yahoo Finance showed US equity futures higher Tuesday morning: S&P futures 7,445.25 (+0.39%), Dow futures 50,961 (+0.21%), Nasdaq futures 29,656.75 (+0.69%), Russell futures 2,881.50 (+0.77%). Crude oil was 89.17 (-2.33%), gold 4,351.90 (-0.26%), 10Y yield 4.5520 (+0.35%), VIX 18.18 (-3.91%). Lead story: OpenAI confidentially filed IPO paperwork after Anthropic did the same a week earlier.
key_points: OpenAI confidential IPO filing after Anthropic
summary: Yahoo Finance market snapshot: US stock futures climb as AI trade takes center stage — Yahoo Finance showed US equity futures higher Tuesday morning: S&P futures 7,445.25 (+0.39%), Dow futures 50,961 (+0.21%), Nasdaq futures 29,656.75 (+0.69%), Ru...

title: Yahoo Finance markets overview
description: Yahoo Finance market overview showed cash/global cross-asset levels: Nasdaq +0.86%, Russell 2000 +0.77%, S&P 500 +0.30%, Dow -0.16%, DXY 99.85 (-0.20%), VIX 18.18 (-3.91%). Europe was mostly higher led by Euronext 100 +0.91% and Euro Stoxx 50 +0.88%; Asia had KOSPI +8.18% and Nikkei +2.17%. Energy fell, with Brent -2.03% and WTI -2.40%; 10Y yield 4.5520 and 30Y 5.02.
summary: Yahoo Finance markets overview — Yahoo Finance market overview showed cash/global cross-asset levels: Nasdaq +0.86%, Russell 2000 +0.77%, S&P 500 +0.30%, Dow -0.16%, DXY 99.85 (-0.20%), VIX 18.18 (-3.91%)

title: Global oil prices erase most gains after topping $98 a barrel
description: MarketWatch reported WTI rose 1.4% to $91.76 after hitting $95.48, while Brent rose 1.6% to $94.55 after briefly topping $98 and gaining more than 5% intraday. Prices erased most gains after Iran signaled its latest operation against Israel had ended, though it warned attacks could resume. OPEC+ approved a 188,000 bpd July production-target increase that analysts said was unlikely to affect prices while Hormuz remained effectively constrained.
summary: Global oil prices erase most gains after topping $98 a barrel — MarketWatch reported WTI rose 1.4% to $91.76 after hitting $95.48, while Brent rose 1.6% to $94.55 after briefly topping $98 and gaining more than 5% intraday
